articles.title: New rules at Oscars gives India a chance
articles.description: Traditionally, this category restricted countries to one submission per year, with specific rules. In order to be considered, a non-English language film must have a theatrical release for seven consecutive days in the country of its origin and be chosen by an organisation, jury or committee approved by the Academy

articles.title: 'Patriot' box office collection day 3: Mohanlal-Mammootty starrer nears big milestone, Overseas response boosts global earnings
articles.description: The Mohanlal-Mammootty starrer 'Patriot' has achieved a remarkable Rs 63.61 crore worldwide in just three days. The film's strong performance in India, especially Kerala, coupled with impressive overseas collections, has propelled it to become this year's highest grosser so far. Audiences and critics are praising the lead actors' performances and the movie's action sequences.

articles.title: 'Dhurandhar- The Revenge' box office collection Day 46: Ranveer Singh starrer beats ‘Baahubali 2', becomes 2nd highest-grossing Indian film ever
articles.description: Aditya Dhar's latest epic, 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge', is making waves in the film industry by becoming the second-highest-grossing Indian film ever, outpacing 'Baahubali 2'. Released in March 2026, this Ranveer Singh-led blockbuster has raked in a jaw-dropping ₹1,788 crore worldwide in just 46 days, demonstrating its box office allure and ability to engage viewers across the globe.

articles.title: The Devil Wears Prada 2 takes top spot in North American box office
articles.description: The sequel sees Anne Hathaway's character return to work under tyrannical editor Miranda Priestly, portrayed again by Meryl Streep, as their fashion magazine faces an existential crisis.

articles.title: Corner Office Conversation: How Guneet Monga Rewrote Bollywood's Rules and Won an Oscar Doing It
articles.description: She grew up navigating a war zone of a family home, arrived in Bombay with ₹50 lakhs borrowed from a neighbour, and watched her debut film get pulled from theatres the morning after India lost the cricket World Cup. That's where most stories end. Guneet Monga's was just beginning. In this candid, far-ranging conversation with ET’s Anirban Chowdhury and in-house film journalist and critic Rajesh Naidu, the Oscar-winning producer and CEO of Sikhya Entertainment traces a 20-year journey defined by jugaad, grit, and an unshakeable belief in the power of story. From Gangs of Wasseypur to The Lunchbox, Kill to Women in Film India — this is the real education they don't teach at film school.

articles.title: Olivia Wilde laughs off 'Gollum' comparisons after weight loss pics go viral: 'It's a startling image'
articles.description: Olivia Wilde addressed viral online criticism of her appearance at a recent red carpet event, attributing the unusual look to a fisheye lens. The actress-director, promoting her film 'The Invite', humorously acknowledged it wasn't her best angle and even shared a light-hearted comparison to Gollum. She playfully dismissed concerns about her well-being, stating, 'I'm not dead.'

articles.title: Shakira's free concert on Brazil's Copacabana beach draws crowd of 2 million
articles.description: Shakira captivated an estimated 2 million fans with a free concert on Rio de Janeiro's Copacabana Beach, part of her "Women No Longer Cry" tour. The Colombian superstar, who first visited Brazil three decades ago, celebrated women's resilience and her enduring connection with the Brazilian public. The event aimed to boost the city's post-Carnival economy, projecting significant financial returns.

articles.title: Quote of the day by Plato: 'Don’t force children into your own ways, as they were created for a time different from your own...' - Student of Socrates teaches the philosophy of how to turn kids into independent thinkers
articles.description: The quote of the day from Plato highlights the importance of accepting generational differences in parenting, suggesting that children should not be forced to follow the same paths as their parents because they grow up in a different time with different realities. It emphasizes that while guidance from parents is important, excessive control can limit a child’s ability to think independently and adapt to the modern world.

articles.title: David Kendall Cause of Death: Boy Meets World Producer Dies at 68
articles.description: David Kendall, the writer, director and executive producer who played a pivotal role in shaping Boy Meets World into one of the most beloved sitcoms of the 1990s, has died at the age of 68.

articles.title: 'The Devil Wears Prada 2' scores USD 233.6 million global debut
articles.description: The highly anticipated sequel, "The Devil Wears Prada 2," has stormed the box office, raking in USD 77 million domestically and USD 156.6 million globally in its opening weekend. Driven primarily by female audiences, the film easily claimed the top spot, outperforming expectations and marking a strong start to Hollywood's summer season. Critics offered mixed reviews, but audience reception remains overwhelmingly positive.

articles.title: Lord of the Rings actor Andy Serkis wants to play Voldemort in HBO's Harry Potter series, says “waiting for the call”
articles.description: Harry Potter is set to return as a television reboot on HBO, with most of the cast already confirmed, though the role of Voldemort is yet to be announced. Actor Andy Serkis has expressed interest in playing the iconic villain, saying he is “waiting for the call” during a recent podcast appearance.

articles.title: Subtle signs you might be vitamin D deficient, and what your body is trying to tell you
articles.description: Vitamin D deficiency often hides behind everyday symptoms like fatigue, low mood, frequent illness, and body aches. These signs may seem unrelated, but together they can point toward a deeper imbalance. With limited sun exposure and modern routines, the risk is higher than before. Recognising early signals and acting on them can prevent long-term health issues.

articles.title: She was fired without warning. What her supervisor did next taught her an important lesson on workplace friendships. ‘There are people who care’
articles.description: A digital creator's unexpected layoff after vacation revealed the emotional toll of corporate restructuring. Witnessing her supervisor's distress and subsequent demotion highlighted the personal impact of impersonal decisions. The experience underscored that while jobs may be temporary, genuine human connections and the care shown by colleagues during difficult times leave a lasting, valuable impression.

articles.title: P.V. Sindhu and Actor Nani laud Pawan Kalyan’s initiatives for rural cleanliness
articles.description: P.V. Sindhu praises Dy CM K. Pawan Kalyan's 'magic drains' initiative, enhancing rural sanitation and transforming village landscapes.

articles.title: 'Raja Shivaji' box office: Riteish Deshmukh's epic rewrites history
articles.description: Riteish Deshmukh's historical epic 'Raja Shivaji' sets a new benchmark in Marathi cinema with a record-breaking opening weekend of Rs 23.90 crore. The film, also directed by Deshmukh, is now the biggest opener ever for a Marathi movie.

articles.title: Shilpa Rao on ‘Chaleya’ challenges; Calls the song in Shah Rukh Khan's film ‘Not easy at all’
articles.description: Playback singer Shilpa Rao, celebrated for her National Award-winning song 'Chaleya' from 'Jawan', discussed the song's recording challenges and her work with director Siddharth Anand. Despite nearly two decades in the industry, Rao admits to still feeling nervous before recordings, a fear she believes keeps her focused and pushes her to perform better.

articles.title: Milly Alcock’s Punk Rock Supergirl Soars As DC Bets Big On Woman Of Tomorrow; N18G
articles.description: Milly Alcock steps into the DC Universe with a bold reinvention of Supergirl in Woman of Tomorrow, portraying a gritty, punk rock hero shaped by loss and survival rather than hope. Backed by DC Studios heads James Gunn and Peter Safran, the film marks a high-stakes chapter in the studio’s rebooted vision, blending emotional depth with a darker, revenge-driven intergalactic journey. With Alcock’s breakout casting and a fresh, anti-hero tone, DC is betting big on redefining its legacy through a fearless new Supergirl. Watch video#supergirl #dcu #millyalcock

articles.title: 'Raja Shivaji' box office collection Day 3 [LIVE]: Riteish Deshmukh starrer is inching towards the Rs 30 crore mark on first Sunday
articles.description: Riteish Deshmukh's ambitious historical drama, 'Raja Shivaji', is captivating audiences in theatres. The film, based on Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj's life, achieved a historic opening for its Marathi version. With a star-studded cast and a special appearance by Salman Khan, the movie has garnered significant box office collections, reaching Rs 23.65 crore net in its initial days despite facing competition. Read on to know more in detail.

articles.title: Rupali Ganguly plays the perfect host at her birthday bash
articles.description: Rupali Ganguly hosted a memorable birthday bash, ensuring every guest felt cherished. The Anupamaa star, surrounded by family and industry friends like Anita Raj and Mohit Malik, cut the cake and personally attended to attendees. A highlight was Rupali dancing with her mother, whose viral moves added to the joyous, food-filled evening.

articles.title: Central Railway launches automobile freight service from Igatpuri Goods Shed
articles.description: Central Railway has launched automobile loading from Igatpuri Goods Shed, boosting vehicle logistics for Nashik's industrial region. The inaugural consignment of 100 cars from Mahindra & Mahindra to Nautanwa signifies a new, efficient rail transport option. This move aims to cut costs, improve supply chains, and support sustainability by shifting freight from highways.

articles.title: Samay Raina roasts Ranveer Allahbadia on The Great Indian Kapil Show, ‘I spent last year thinking about his death’
articles.description: Samay Raina and Ranveer Allahbadia reunited on The Great Indian Kapil Show, where their banter quickly turned into a playful roast. Samay took multiple digs at Ranveer, delivering sharp one-liners that became the highlight of the episode.
